title: Panic when trying to run or start a container
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Unable to start containers (even the ones that were working previously) after upgrading from version `3.4.6` to `3.4.7`.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. In Debain Sid upgrade podman to its latest version (at the time of writing this issue version `3.4.7+ds1-2`).
2. Try to start any container, e.g: `podman run envoyproxy/envoy:v1.20.0` or `podman start <name-of-an-already-exising-but-stopped-container>`

**Describe the results you received:**

```
panic: assignment to entry in nil map

goroutine 1 [running]:
github.com/opencontainers/runtime-tools/generate.(*Generator).addEnv(...)
	github.com/opencontainers/runtime-tools/generate/generate.go:532
github.com/opencontainers/runtime-tools/generate.(*Generator).AddProcessEnv(0xc00056f0d8, {0x17edeb7, 0x8}, {0xc0001d5c40, 0xc})
	github.com/opencontainers/runtime-tools/generate/generate.go:508 +0x2ea
github.com/containers/podman/libpod.(*Container).generateSpec(0xc0001440b0, {0x1ad3150, 0xc000630ab0})
	github.com/containers/podman/libpod/container_internal_linux.go:648 +0x2965
github.com/containers/podman/libpod.(*Container).init(0xc0001440b0, {0x1ad3150, 0xc000630ab0}, 0x0)
	github.com/containers/podman/libpod/container_internal.go:1098 +0x8e
github.com/containers/podman/libpod.(*Container).prepareToStart(0xc0001440b0, {0x1ad3150, 0xc000630ab0}, 0xc0?)
	github.com/containers/podman/libpod/container_internal.go:875 +0x345
github.com/containers/podman/libpod.(*Container).Start(0xc0001440b0, {0x1ad3150?, 0xc000630ab0?}, 0x0?)
	github.com/containers/podman/libpod/container_api.go:91 +0xde
github.com/containers/podman/pkg/domain/infra/abi.(*ContainerEngine).ContainerStart(0xc000010038, {0x1ad3150, 0xc000630ab0}, {0xc000640a00?, 0x7?, 0xc00056fd30?}, {0xc0002a65d0, 0x0, 0x0, {0x17f5ed4, ...}, ...})
	github.com/containers/podman/pkg/domain/infra/abi/containers.go:856 +0x36b
github.com/containers/podman/cmd/podman/containers.start(0x2504e20?, {0xc000640a00, 0x2, 0x2})
	github.com/containers/podman/cmd/podman/containers/start.go:136 +0x1f4
github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).execute(0x2504e20, {0xc00003c0e0, 0x2, 0x2})
	github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:856 +0x67c
github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).ExecuteC(0x251b3a0)
	github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:974 +0x3b4
github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).Execute(...)
	github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:902
github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).ExecuteContext(...)
	github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:895
main.Execute()
	github.com/containers/podman/cmd/podman/root.go:91 +0xc5
main.main()
	github.com/containers/podman/cmd/podman/main.go:39 +0x74
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

I expected for the container to start.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

As I said earlier, I'm using Debian Sid. Issue started when I upgraded from `3.4.6+ds1-1` to `3.4.7+ds1-2`. I downgraded to `3.4.6+ds1-1` by adding Debian Testing source and the issue was fixed.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 3.4.7
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman/unstable,now 3.4.7+ds1-2 amd64 [installed]
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
